This type of salt is also known as a complex salt or a coordination compound, and it has unique physical and chemical properties that are different from simple salts.

questions.text: When FeCl3 reacts with K3[Fe(CN)6] no blue colour is observed. K3[Fe(CN)6] is used as an indicator in the estimation of Fe2+ by Cr2O7 2– ion in an acidic medium. End point will be when:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Solution of iron salt does not give blue colour with indicator.
options.explanation: When FeCl3 reacts with K3[Fe(CN)6], no blue color is observed because the Fe3+ ion in FeCl3 oxidizes the Fe2+ ion in K3[Fe(CN)6] to Fe3+ ion, which does not form a blue complex with the [Fe(CN)6]3- ion. K3[Fe(CN)6] is used as an indicator in the estimation of Fe2+ by Cr2O7 2– ion in an acidic medium because the blue complex formed between Fe2+ and [Fe(CN)6]3- is oxidized by Cr2O7 2– ion to form Fe3+ ion, which does not form a blue complex. The end point of the titration is reached when all the Fe2+ ions have been oxidized to Fe3+ ions, and the blue color of the complex disappears. At this point, the amount of Fe2+ ions can be calculated from the volume and concentration of the Cr2O7 2– ion used in the titration. The endpoint is usually indicated by a color change from blue to colorless.

questions.text: The compound [Co(NO2)(NH3)5]Cl2 and [Co(ONO)(NH3)5]Cl2 are examples of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The compounds [Co(NO2)(NH3)5]Cl2 and [Co(ONO)(NH3)5]Cl2 are not geometrical isomers, but rather linkage isomers. Geometrical isomers occur when there are two different ways in which the ligands can be arranged around the central metal ion, while linkage isomers occur when the same ligand is bonded to the metal ion through different atoms. In the case of these two compounds, the nitrite (NO2) and nitro (ONO) ligands are different, but the arrangement of the ligands around the central metal ion is the same.

The compounds [Co(NO2)(NH3)5]Cl2 and [Co(ONO)(NH3)5]Cl2 are examples of coordination compounds or transition metal complexes. Coordination compounds are molecules that consist of a central metal ion surrounded by a group of ligands. Ligands are molecules or ions that can donate a pair of electrons to the central metal ion. In these two compounds, the central metal ion is cobalt (Co), and the ligands are nitrite (NO2) and nitro (ONO) ions, and five ammonia (NH3) molecules. The coordination number of the central metal ion in both compounds is six, which means that it is surrounded by six ligands. The Cl- ions in the compounds are not directly bonded to the central metal ion, but they are present to balance the charge of the complex. These two compounds are also examples of isomers. They are known as linkage isomers, which means that the ligand is bonded to the central metal ion through different atoms. In [Co(NO2)(NH3)5]Cl2, the NO2 ligand is bonded to the Co ion through the nitrogen atom, while in [Co(ONO)(NH3)5]Cl2, the ONO ligand is bonded to the Co ion through the oxygen atom.

[Co(NO2)(NH3)5]Cl2 and [Co(ONO)(NH3)5]Cl2 are examples of linkage isomers. Linkage isomers occur when the same ligand is bonded to the metal ion through different atoms. In these two compounds, the nitrite (NO2) and nitro (ONO) ligands are bonded to the cobalt ion through different atoms. In [Co(NO2)(NH3)5]Cl2, the NO2 ligand is bonded to the cobalt ion through the nitrogen atom, while in [Co(ONO)(NH3)5]Cl2, the ONO ligand is bonded to the cobalt ion through the oxygen atom.

Ionization isomers are a type of coordination isomerism in which the counterion in a complex salt is replaced with a different ion. This results in two different isomers with the same chemical formula but different properties. For example, [Co(NH3)5SO4]Br and [Co(NH3)5Br]SO4 are ionization isomers. In the first compound, the counterion is SO4, while in the second compound, the counterion is Br.

questions.text: The colour of transition metal complexes is due to:

questions.explanation: The color of transition metal complexes is primarily due to d-d transitions. In these complexes, the presence of a partially filled d subshell allows electrons to transition between different d orbitals. When light is absorbed, electrons are promoted to higher energy levels, and the specific wavelengths absorbed correspond to the color observed. This is why transition metals with no unpaired d electrons, such as Sc+3, Ti+4, V+5, Cu+1, and Zn+2, do not display the characteristic d-d transitions and are often colorless.Option B is incorrect because the paramagnetic nature of transition metals is related to unpaired electrons affecting magnetic properties rather than color. Option C is incorrect because ionization involves electron removal, not affecting the color directly. Option D is incorrect as the loss of s-electrons pertains to oxidation states rather than the origin of color in complexes.

questions.text: Which of the following is incorrect information?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Metals have free electrons in the valence shell
options.explanation: This is correct. Metals have a few valence electrons that are relatively loosely bound to the atomic structure, allowing them to move freely and conduct electricity.

options.text: The electrical conductivity of metals increases with the increase in temperature
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect because the electrical conductivity of metals decreases with an increase in temperature, not increases. This phenomenon is known as thermal resistivity or positive temperature coefficient of resistance. As the temperature of a metal increases, the vibrations of atoms and lattice defects increase, leading to more collisions with the free electrons responsible for electrical conduction. These collisions impede the flow of electrons, resulting in higher resistance and decreased electrical conductivity.

options.text: Metalloids are poor conductors of electricity
options.explanation: This is generally correct. Metalloids, also known as semimetals, have properties intermediate between metals and non-metals. They can exhibit varying degrees of electrical conductivity, depending on the specific material and conditions.

options.text: Non-metals have atomic conductance usually less than 10-10 ohm-1
options.explanation: This is correct. Non-metals generally have much higher resistivity and lower electrical conductivity compared to metals. Their atomic conductance is indeed typically much lower than 10-10 ohm-1.

questions.explanation: Statement B is incorrect because the electrical conductivity of metals decreases with an increase in temperature, not increases. These collisions impede the flow of electrons, resulting in higher resistance and decreased electrical conductivity.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is H2O2 because it decomposes to release oxygen gas. It also turns an acidic solution of KI brown by oxidizing I- to I2 and reduces KMnO4 in an acidic medium, leading to the formation of MnO2 and the reduction of the oxidation state of Mn from +7 to +4.
